item.title: Citi Analyst Blasted by Indonesia Says Fiscal Risks Have Peaked
item.description: Fiscal and currency risks have likely passed in Indonesia, according to a Citigroup Inc. analyst who was once lambasted by the country’s finance chief for his forecasts.

item.title: Pictet, Neuberger to Boost India Bond Exposure on Policy Support
item.description: Global funds are piling into Indian government bonds after New Delhi removed taxes on debt for foreign investors and eased ownership caps, along with moves that have helped stabilize the rupee.

item.title: NSE Filing Puts India IPO Market Back in Focus
item.description: NSE is currently valued at about $53 billion in the unlisted market.

item.title: What’s at Stake If Indonesia Loses Its Emerging Market Status
item.description: Indonesia has spent decades establishing itself as one of the world’s most important emerging markets. Now the country — Southeast Asia’s largest economy, with a gross domestic product of about $1.5 trillion — risks losing that standing, potentially jeopardizing billions of dollars in foreign investment.

item.title: China’s Tilt to Bonds From Loans Gives PBOC Broader Easing Tool
item.description: The increasingly large role of China’s bond market as a source of credit for the nation’s borrowers has offered the central bank an important channel for broad reductions in borrowing costs.

item.title: Lululemon Apologizes After Great Wall Drum Sparks China Backlash
item.description: Lululemon Athletica Inc. apologized after a yoga event staged on the Great Wall featuring a Japanese taiko drum sparked social media backlash in China, highlighting the reputational risks facing the company in its fastest-growing major market.

item.title: Goldman’s Kaplan Says Fed May Hike by Fall, Sees Risk of More
item.description: The Federal Reserve may need to raise interest rates as soon as September if inflation remains elevated, according to Rob Kaplan, vice chairman at Goldman Sachs Group Inc. and former Dallas Fed president.

item.title: LNG Tanker Heads Toward Hormuz as US-Iran Pact Goes Into Effect
item.description: A liquefied natural gas tanker that loaded in Qatar is nearing the Strait of Hormuz, a hint that traffic may increase after an interim US-Iran peace deal went into effect.

item.title: Hillhouse Seeks $600 Million Loan to Fund DayOne Investment
item.description: Asset manager Hillhouse Investment is in talks with banks for a loan of about $600 million to fund its investment in data center operator DayOne Data Centers Ltd., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Lee Says Korea-US Ties ‘Eternal’ as Trump Invites Him to Golf
item.description: South Korean President Lee Jae Myung said his country’s relationship with the US is “solid and eternal” after he spoke with President Donald Trump on the sidelines of the Group of Seven summit in France.

item.title: China AI Lab’s 170% Stock Surge Cements Winner-Loser Pair Trade
item.description: A pair trade is emerging in China’s artificial intelligence sector, with investors piling into the perceived winner and betting against its rival seen as losing ground in the race to build commercially viable AI models.

item.title: Banxico Publishes Rules to Boost Digital Payments, Bank Accounts
item.description: Mexico’s central bank published on Wednesday new rules to simplify digital payments as well as increase the deposit limits on accounts for small businesses as part of efforts to curb cash dealings in the country.

item.title: Heart Health Biotech Kardigan Raises $400 Million in Upsized IPO
item.description: Kardigan Inc. raised $400 million in an upsized US initial public offering that priced at the top of its marketed range.

item.title: Malaysia and Russia Weigh Using Ruble, Ringgit to Boost Trade
item.description: Malaysian Prime Minister Anwar Ibrahim and Russian President Vladimir Putin discussed using local currencies in bilateral trade as part of efforts to strengthen economic ties.

item.title: Channel Buys Fidante to Grow Australia Fund Manager Services
item.description: Channel Capital will merge with Challenger Ltd.’s Fidante unit to create an Australian fund manager spanning a range of investment strategies with around A$150 billion ($105 billion) in assets.

item.title: Short Seller Left Loses Mistrial Bid Over Court Error, for Now
item.description: Famed short seller Andrew Left, convicted on 13 counts of securities fraud earlier this month, is off to a rocky start in his effort to sway a judge to overturn his verdict due to an unusual courtroom error.

item.title: China’s Unusually Heavy Rains Fill Dams and Put Crops at Risk
item.description: It’s been a particularly wet start to southern China’s rainy season, and more unusually heavy downpours could be on the way over the summer.

item.title: Braskem Debt Talks Hit Snag, Boosting Odds of Emergency Order
item.description: Braskem SA and its new controlling shareholder, IG4 Capital, are struggling to win support from enough creditors to move forward with an out-of-court restructuring proposal amid disagreements over uneven treatment and collaterals, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Traders Keep Faith in Malaysian Bonds in Face of Deficit Warning
item.description: Traders remain sanguine about Malaysia’s financial outlook even after officials warned the government may miss its deficit target this year, according to a closely watched financial-market metric.

item.title: Asia Strategists Eye Yen Intervention, Tech Stocks Post-Warsh
item.description: Strategists say markets are on watch for yen intervention after the Federal Reserve took a hawkish stance at Kevin Warsh’s debut meeting as governor, triggering a drop in the currency to levels that have previously prompted Japan’s finance ministry to step in.

item.title: Fed’s Warsh Signals Revamp, But Offers Little Substance on Rates
item.description: Kevin Warsh began his tenure as chairman of the Federal Reserve with a solemn vow to curb inflation and a clear sign that he plans to swiftly revamp how the US central bank does its job.

item.title: Multinationals Shield Ireland From Iran Shock, Central Bank Says
item.description: Multinational companies are buoying Irish economic growth even as conflict in the Middle East dampens consumer demand, according to the Central Bank of Ireland.

item.title: BOE Set to Hold Rates With Energy Shock Receding: Decision Guide
item.description: The Bank of England is expected to hold interest rates on Thursday as inflation proves less of a threat than feared and energy costs fall on hopes for an end to the conflict in the Middle East.

item.title: Indonesia, Philippines Set to Hike Rates as Caution Prevails
item.description: Two of Asia’s beleaguered central banks are poised to tighten monetary policy further, staying on guard in case inflation and currency risks persist despite a potential deescalation of the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Taiwan Seen Holding Benchmark Rate Even as Inflation Speeds Up
item.description: Taiwan’s central bank is expected to leave its benchmark interest rate unchanged for a ninth straight quarter, though a roaring economy and accelerating inflation are creating pressure for it to join other Asian economies in turning hawkish.

item.title: Gold Holds Decline After Bets Increase on Fed Hike This Year
item.description: Gold held a loss after the US Federal Reserve kept interest rates unchanged and signaled a hike later in the year.

item.title: Fed’s Warsh Earns Points From Economists for ‘Slick’ Performance
item.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh’s highly anticipated maiden press conference on Wednesday was, according to many Fed watchers, smooth, polished and tightly controlled. Following the decision to hold rates steady, Warsh gave a statement and fielded questions from reporters.

item.title: New Zealand’s Economy Accelerated Before War Sapped Momentum
item.description: New Zealand’s economy sparked into life in the first three months of the year, responding to low interest rates and a lift in spending that largely pre-dated the Iran war.

item.title: BWX Agrees to License Nuclear Reactor Design After Activist Push
item.description: BWX Technologies Inc. has struck a deal to license its design for a small modular reactor, after activist investor Ananym Capital Management pushed the nuclear technology company to consider commercializing it.

item.title: Pimco Favors Australian Bonds, Betting on Rate Cuts Next Year
item.description: Pacific Investment Management Co. is favoring five- to 10-year Australian debt, wagering the central bank will ultimately be forced to pivot to interest rate cuts to support a slowing economy.
